items.title: Can Global Stocks Continue to Outperform the US in 2025?
items.author: Charles-Henry Monchau
items.description: Global stocks have outperformed US stocks so far in 2025, with international stocks up 5.8% compared to a 2.9% gain for the S&P 500. Investors are also pouring money into companies expected to benefit from Ukraine's post-war reconstruction efforts, as well as gold and gold mining stocks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions Strabag SE as an Austrian construction company that is expected to play a significant role in Ukraine's post-war reconstruction efforts.

Wienerberger AG, a company specializing in residential real estate construction and related materials, is mentioned as having significant exposure in countries neighboring Ukraine and is expected to be involved in the reconstruction efforts.

JPMorgan Chase is collaborating with BlackRock and McKinsey to establish a fund aimed at attracting private investment for Ukraine's reconstruction, indicating a positive outlook for the company's involvement.

The article suggests that gold mining companies could be the biggest beneficiaries of the favorable situation for gold, as production costs have risen only about $100-$200/oz while gold prices have increased by nearly $1,000/oz, implying a positive sentiment towards gold miners.

items.title: J.P. Morgan Asset Management Announces Liquidation of JPMorgan Sustainable Infrastructure ETF (BLLD)
items.description: NEW YORK, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- J.P. Morgan Asset Management today announced the upcoming liquidation and dissolution of the JPMorgan Sustainable Infrastructure ETF . ...
